title: bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. fluviatilis
intro: Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. fluviatilis, commonly known as river club-rush, is a plant species of the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of sedge that grows in wet environments, such as rivers and streams. The health benefits of river club-rush include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects.

This plant is also known to have antioxidant properties, helping to protect against cell damage and potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers. Its extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective in wound healing and preventing the spread of infections. By incorporating Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. fluviatilis into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall health, enhanced well-being, and a better quality of life.

side_effects: Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. fluviatilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high fiber content. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems like asthma. Furthermore, improper use of Bolboschoenus maritimus subsp. fluviatilis can lead to kidney problems and interact with certain medications, like diuretics and blood thinners.
